motivation: Gemma 4 26B-A4B with V_max 10.22x remained safe under naive4 (21/21 NIAH), contradicting a simple 8x outlier threshold. Investigated whether K and V outliers have asymmetric impact on naive INT4 quantization.

gemma_4_31b.note: NEW RECORD V outlier (2.4x higher than prior max of Mistral-7B). Still safe -- decisive evidence for K/V asymmetry hypothesis.

v_outliers_tolerated.evidence: Gemma 4 31B V_max 39.63x at Layer 12 remains safe (21/21 NIAH). Prior: Mistral-7B V_max 16.47x at Layer 0 also safe. V outlier tolerance extends to at least 39.63x in our sample.
v_outliers_tolerated.mechanism: V is used only in the weighted-sum readout of attention. Per-row quantization errors in V cause noisy values at the correct attention locations, but do not corrupt attention structure itself.

late_layer_k_outliers_tolerated.evidence: Qwen2.5-14B K_max 10.65x at Layer 30 (of 48) remains safe (26/26 NIAH).
late_layer_k_outliers_tolerated.mechanism: K outliers at late layers only affect ~18 subsequent layers, limiting multiplicative error propagation.

pathological_pattern_identified.description: Catastrophic naive4 failure requires K outlier magnitude >15x AND concentration at Layer 0.
pathological_pattern_identified.evidence: Only Qwen2-7B in our 5-model sample matches this pattern (K 17.23x at Layer 0). The resulting error propagates through all 27 subsequent attention layers, causing complete breakdown (0/26 NIAH).
pathological_pattern_identified.mechanism: K determines attention scoring (softmax(QK^T/sqrt(d))); errors at Layer 0 propagate multiplicatively through the entire network depth.

hypothesis_evaluation.simple_8x_threshold: REFUTED -- Qwen2.5-14B (K 10.65x, safe) and Mistral-7B (V 16.47x, safe) violate the prediction
hypothesis_evaluation.refined_hypothesis: Catastrophic failure requires specific combination of high K magnitude (>15x) AND early-layer concentration (Layer 0). Observed in 1 of 5 models.

implications_for_paper: No single numeric threshold reliably predicts naive4 failure.
V outlier magnitude is not a reliable indicator (up to 16.47x tolerated).
K outlier magnitude alone is insufficient (10.65x at Layer 30 tolerated).
nsep+pchan4 as universal safety net: zero overhead on safe models, full recovery on pathological ones.
Per-channel quantization isolates outlier channels, preventing scale domination that corrupts other dimensions.
The attention mechanism's K/V asymmetry explains why per-channel quantization specifically targets the harmful pattern.
